description_long: This is a tall, ornate ceramic vase with a wide flared mouth and a bulbous body, finished in a pale turquoise glaze. The neck and upper body are adorned with intricate, colorful reliefs of birds and flowers, rendered in pastel pinks, greens, and yellows. Around the widest part of the body, there are raised, lattice-like beadwork patterns, giving the surface a textured, almost studded appearance. Two large, circular medallions with detailed floral and bird motifs are set into the sides, bordered in gold. The vase's base is slightly narrower, providing stability, and the overall craftsmanship suggests a decorative, possibly antique or traditional Asian style. The surface is glossy, reflecting light and highlighting the fine details of the ornamentation.
description: A tall, pale turquoise ceramic vase with ornate reliefs of birds and flowers, raised beadwork, and gold-bordered medallions, finished with a glossy sheen.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flared mouth, detailed bird and flower reliefs, and a prominent gold-bordered medallion with floral motifs.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the textured beadwork and the transition from the medallion to the plain, decorated surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view features the second gold-bordered medallion and a clear view of the intricate reliefs on the neck.
description_view_3: Back view emphasizes the continuous beadwork and floral reliefs, with the medallion partially visible on the side.
attribute_pattern: floral and bird motifs with lattice beadwork
